methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $274M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.5B.

data.narrative:
Sift, a Series B space and aerospace company founded by Karthik Gollapudi and Austin Spiegel, has raised $65M to date against a last post-money valuation of $274M. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 8 private peers at same or adjacent stages (including Aalyria at $1.3B, Impulse Space at $1.8B, and Axiom Space at $2.5B) and 5 public Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier applied to the $274M post-money, implies $521M. Peer-median anchoring across the 8 same-sector comparables yields $1.2B, with a range of $400M to $2.5B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data. 

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $443M (low), $873M (median), and $1.4B (high). The median reflects material upside from current post-money valuation, consistent with stage-typical expansion. Key uncertainties include the width of the peer range ($400M–$2.5B spread) and revenue trajectory assumptions implicit in peer comparisons. The public-comp method remains unavailable pending disclosure of Sift's revenue or near-term guidance.
